Guideline: Formal register. Uses siz (formal you) consistently. Verbs in formal second person plural.
Turkish is naturally gender-neutral; no specific gender markers needed.
Turkish capitalization: proper nouns and sentence beginnings. Note Turkish-specific dotted/dotless I distinction.
Standard double quotes for feature names.
Standard Turkish punctuation. Colons for labels.
Turkish agglutination: compound terms built with suffixes. Davranis kitapliklari, Ucretlendirme ayarlari.

Some kept: Check-in, Analytics Hub, Data Foundation, API, CSV, SFTP. Generally translated: Widget -> pencere ogesi (window element); Feedback -> Geri bildirimler; Chat -> Sohbet; PII -> fully spelled out as Kisisel olarak tanimlanabilir bilgi.
Imperative formal: Yayinla (publish), Iptal Et (cancel), Yenile (refresh). Instructions: Secin..., Tanimlayin...
Enable: X ozelligini etkinlestirin or X pencere ogesini etkinlestirin. Confirmations: ...istediginizden emin misiniz? Success: ...basariyla... Empty states: Henuz ... yok.

Check-in=Check-in gorusmesi (adds meeting); Review=Degerlendirme; Goal=Hedef; Worker=Calisan; Manager=Yonetici; Compensation=Ucretlendirme; Behavior=Davranis; Widget=Pencere ogesi; Career Development=Kariyer Gelisimi; Settings=Ayarlar; Library=Kitaplik; PII=Kisisel olarak tanimlanabilir bilgi
